2025-05-25.title: Qatari Push to Dominate Another Sport, Table Tennis, Draws Scrutiny
2025-05-25.abstract: A wealthy Qatari businessman’s campaign to lead the sport’s global body is being investigated. One of his critics was detained and interrogated in Doha.

2025-05-25.title: ​North Korea Makes Arrests Over Failed Ship Launch That Angered Kim Jong-un
2025-05-25.abstract: Three shipyard officials were arrested, including the chief engineer, state media said. The North Korean leader was watching as the launch of a navy destroyer went wrong.

2025-05-25.title: Selfies From the Front: Inside the ‘V.I.P.’ Brigade for Ambitious Russian Officials
2025-05-25.abstract: After a stint with a drone unit led by a member of Parliament, Russian officials return to work, and promotions, garlanded as war veterans. Most soldiers are stuck in indefinite deployments.

2025-05-25.title: Trump’s Warming Toward Syria Complicates Israel’s Military Strategy
2025-05-25.abstract: Since President Trump’s embrace of the new Syrian leader, Israeli airstrikes on the country have subsided.

2025-05-26.title: As South Korea Gets Ready to Vote, Women Don’t Like the Choices
2025-05-26.abstract: Women took the lead in the protests against South Korea’s last president. But the men running to replace him are saying little about the discrimination they face.

2025-05-26.title: Venezuela’s Government Claims Victory in Polls Boycotted by Opposition Leader
2025-05-26.abstract: Polling places in Caracas, the capital, and other cities were sparsely populated but officials claimed turnout was higher than 40 percent.

2025-05-26.title: Head of New Gaza Aid System Resigns Over Lack of Autonomy
2025-05-26.abstract: Jake Wood quit the Gaza Humanitarian Foundation, saying it was “not possible” to implement a new Israeli-backed aid system in the enclave while remaining neutral and independent.

2025-05-26.title: This Italian Port City Transformed Its Identity. Here Come the Tourists.
2025-05-26.abstract: Foreigners — and residents — long avoided Bari’s old town, which had been plagued by mob crime. Now, with direct flights from the U.S. starting, residents are excited, but wary.

2025-05-26.title: Risking Their Lives to ‘Self-Deport’
2025-05-26.abstract: Thousands of Venezuelan migrants are doing just what U.S. leaders want them to. But to get home, they face as many dangers as on the journey north.

2025-05-27.title: A Science Fiction Writer Wrestles With China’s Rise, and His Own Decline
2025-05-27.abstract: In his stories, Han Song explores the disorientation accompanying China’s modernization, sometimes writing of unthinkable things that later came true.

2025-05-27.title: How North Korea Botched the Launch of a Warship
2025-05-27.abstract: Satellite images and videos show how launching a 5,000-ton warship into the water sideways can cause it to capsize if done incorrectly.

2025-05-27.title: Police Quickly Gave Details on Liverpool Car Ramming, Aiming to Prevent Rumors
2025-05-27.abstract: Just hours after the episode, the police announced that the driver was a white British man. After previous violence, false anti-Muslim speculation had been spread online.

2025-05-27.title: In Nashville, Volunteers Are Figuring Out How to Counter ICE
2025-05-27.abstract: In early May, Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents were spotted alongside Tennessee State Highway Patrol troopers in areas of Nashville.

2025-05-27.title: Israeli Forces Said They Killed a ‘Terrorist.’ He Was 14 Years Old.
2025-05-27.abstract: The death of Amer Rabee, a Palestinian American in the West Bank, has spurred anger over soldiers’ use of force and an apparent lack of accountability.

2025-05-28.title: Germany Says It Will Step Up Weapons Support for Ukraine
2025-05-28.abstract: In the latest sign of a warming relationship, President Volodymyr Zelensky met in Berlin with Chancellor Friedrich Merz, who said Germany would increase funding for arms production and supplies.

2025-05-28.title: Stalin’s Image Returns to Moscow’s Subway, Honoring a Brutal History
2025-05-28.abstract: The Kremlin has increasingly embraced the Soviet dictator and his legacy, using them to exalt Russian history in a time of war, but he remains a deeply divisive figure in Russia.

2025-05-28.title: A Desperate Haiti Turns to Erik Prince, Trump Ally, in Fight Against Gangs
2025-05-28.abstract: The Haitian government has signed a contract with Mr. Prince, the private military contractor who founded Blackwater, a company notorious for a civilian massacre in Iraq.

2025-05-28.title: Israeli Jets Strike Yemen’s Main Airport Again After Houthi Attacks
2025-05-28.abstract: Israel said the bombing of the airport, which was targeted for the second time this month, had destroyed the last plane used by the Iran-backed Houthi militia.

2025-05-28.title: French Doctor Who Sexually Abused Hundreds of Children Is Sentenced
2025-05-28.abstract: A former surgeon had confessed to abusing at least 299 people, mostly children, in what is considered the largest case of its kind in French history.

2025-05-29.title: U.K. Weighs Sanctions on 2 Israeli Cabinet Ministers as Gaza Crisis Worsens
2025-05-29.abstract: Facing rising pressure over the acute suffering of civilians in Gaza, the British government is considering sanctions on two far-right ministers, Itamar Ben-Gvir and Bezalel Smotrich.

2025-05-29.title: Four Killed as South Korean Navy Airplane Crashes Into Hillside
2025-05-29.abstract: The authorities are investigating what caused the accident involving a turboprop patrol plane.

2025-05-29.title: Chinese Students Are Frustrated With U.S. Visa Bans: ‘What Now?’
2025-05-29.abstract: As applicants in Beijing wait to see how sweeping the new action might be, one says the United States is making people too scared.

2025-05-29.title: Far From Home: Uyghur Workers in Factories Supplying Global Brands
2025-05-29.abstract: China’s persecution of Uyghurs prompted the U.S. to ban Xinjiang imports. China found a way around it — by shipping more Uyghurs across the country.

2025-05-29.title: What to Know About Mexico’s Sweeping Judicial Election
2025-05-29.abstract: For the first time, Mexicans will vote for thousands of candidates to fill more than 2,600 positions in federal, state and local courts.

2025-05-30.title: Under Pressure From China, Myanmar Rebels Cede Hard-Won Territory
2025-05-30.abstract: Beijing has intervened significantly in Myanmar’s civil war to protect its substantial investments in the country, handing a setback to resistance forces.

2025-05-30.title: In South Korea, Desperate Workers Take Their Grievances Into the Sky
2025-05-30.abstract: The plight of temporary workers like Kim Hyoung-su, who’s been protesting for months at the top of a tower, has become an issue in Tuesday’s presidential election.

2025-05-30.title: 200 Miles of Sublime Pain on a Hindu Pilgrimage in Pakistan
2025-05-30.abstract: In 113-degree heat, thousands of Hindus in Pakistan make a grueling trek that is an act of spiritual devotion and cultural preservation in a Muslim-majority country.

2025-05-30.title: The U.S.-Canada Border Runs Through This Library. That’s Now a Problem.
2025-05-30.abstract: A wealthy widow built a library and opera house on the border between Quebec and Vermont, a symbol of binational friendship. Now U.S. officials are restricting access to Canadians.

2025-05-30.title: Afrikaners See Trump as the Ally Who ‘Heard Our Cries’
2025-05-30.abstract: Thousands of white South Africans are jockeying to get on the next flight to the United States as refugees. They say the backlash against their community is unfair.

2025-05-31.title: World Scientists Look Elsewhere as U.S. Labs Stagger Under Trump Cuts
2025-05-31.abstract: With the welcome mat withdrawn for promising researchers from around the world, America is at risk of losing its longstanding pre-eminence in the sciences.

2025-05-31.title: India and Pakistan’s Air Battle Is Over. Their Water War Has Begun.
2025-05-31.abstract: Though the two neighbors declared a cease-fire this month, a crucial water-sharing treaty remains at risk.

2025-05-31.title: In Poland, Presidential Hopefuls Battle for Young Voters Who Don’t Like Them
2025-05-31.abstract: A runoff election on Sunday pits a centrist against a nationalist who represent mainstream parties. The outcome is unclear given that many under 30 prefer candidates on the political fringes.

2025-05-31.title: Hegseth, at Security Forum, Vows to Strengthen America’s Asian Alliances
2025-05-31.abstract: In a world rattled by President Trump’s tariffs and his scorn for Western allies, the defense secretary struck a rare note of continuity on security partnerships.

2025-05-31.title: How Mexico’s Vote on Nearly 2,700 Judges Could Empower One Party
2025-05-31.abstract: The election to overhaul Mexico’s courts could result in a justice system more beholden to the nation’s dominant party, Morena.
